text.title: market-research-tam-sam-som Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for market-research-tam-sam-som. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: For B2B markets, use government establishment data (US Census BDS, Eurostat) as the top-down customer count anchor — it is free and primary.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn Sales Navigator counts are inaccessible for pipelines; Statista customer counts are paywalled. Government data is free, primary, and reliable for establishment counts by NAICS/NACE code.

rule.statement: Run all three methods independently before triangulation. Never run bottom-up after reviewing top-down results — anchoring bias inflates the bottom-up estimate.
rule.rationale: If the agent sees a $10B top-down TAM before computing the bottom-up, it will unconsciously reverse-engineer to produce a consistent number. Independence requires sequential blind computation.

rule.statement: If methods diverge by more than 2x, do NOT average — report the range and flag the divergence. Investigate the outlier method before reconciling.
rule.rationale: Averaging incompatible methods produces false precision. A 2x spread signals that one method's assumptions are wrong; averaging hides the problem and misleads investors.

rule.statement: Lock the ICP definition in one sentence and wait for explicit confirmation before fetching any number. "All SMBs" is not an ICP.
rule.rationale: Agents computing TAM/SAM/SOM without a locked ICP will scope to different customer segments across the three methods, making triangulation impossible and producing numbers that cannot be compared.
